email.subject: Nassau University Medical Center — infant follow-up is working; maternal discharge prep lags
email.email_body:
Hi,

While 91.5% of newborns in NY make it to their well-baby visits, many mothers fall through the cracks after discharge. At Nassau University Medical Center, only 69% of patients report receiving the information they need for recovery, and your care transition rating sits at just 1 star.

This gap matters because maternal outcomes directly impact your Birthing-Friendly designation and patient experience scores. Remote postpartum monitoring can help bridge this divide—BP monitoring kits, a mobile app, and OB-specialized care managers working together to keep moms engaged after they leave.

Hospitals using Babyscripts saw patients become 2x more likely to complete their 30-day postpartum visit.

Would you be open to a 15-minute call to explore how this might fit into your current maternal care workflow?

email.subject: Healthalliance Hospital Marys Avenue Campus — infant follow-up is working; maternal discharge prep lags
email.email_body:
Hi,

You've earned the CMS Birthing-Friendly designation—that's meaningful work. Yet after delivery, the picture shifts. While well-baby visits track at 91.5% statewide in NY, postpartum care completion tells a different story.

Right now, 73% of patients at Healthalliance Hospital Marys Avenue Campus report receiving the discharge information they need for recovery. That gap compounds: mothers who don't get clear recovery guidance are less likely to show up for their own 30-day postpartum visit, even as their newborns stay on track.

Hospitals using Babyscripts saw patients become 2x more likely to complete their 30-day postpartum visit. The service wraps remote monitoring—BP kit, mobile app, OB-specialized care managers—around discharge planning so mothers stay connected during those critical first weeks.
